titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 713

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=75 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.748189+00 | translation moa=76 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.734997+00

p.cl: Willelmus de lindeseie . Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis salutem.

Notum sit omnibus uidentibus uel audientibus has litteras
me quantum ad me spectat sicut ad dominum fundi dedisse ⁊ hac Carta confirmasse deo ⁊ sancto Cuthberto ⁊ mo
nachis eidem sancto in dunelmo seruientibus Ecclesiam de ærcheldun cum una carrucata terre ⁊ omnibus aliis perti
nentiis suis in puram ⁊ perpetuam Elemosinam . Quare volo quod idem Monachi predictam Ecclesiam ⁊ omnia illi
adiacentia libere ⁊ quiete ⁊ integre possideant inperpetuum . ⁊ muluram suam ommino quietam habeant ⁊ quicunque
predictam Ecclesiam de ipsis tenuerit . Promisi qutem quod pro posse meo hanc elemosinam ad opus ⁊ utilita
tem Ecclesie dunelmensis Manutenebo .

Hiis testibus . Johanne archidiacono dunelmensi . alano presbitero de Walesende
Magistro thoma . Andrea archidiacono laodoneie . Magistro Ricardo de Cold’ . patricio decano herueio ⁊ Rompha
ro presbiteris . Magistro thoma de lambertun . Ricardo capellano archidiaconi . Ricardo persona de tuedemuthe . Ricardo
filio herueii . waltero dapifero . adam de walesende . helia de walesende ⁊ aliis multis .
William of Lindsay, to all sons of Holy Mother Church, greeting.

Let it be known to all who see or hear these letters that, inasmuch as it pertains to me as to the possessor, I have given and by this my charter made firm, to God, Saint Cuthbert, and to the monks of the same saint serving God in Durham, the Church of Earlston, with one ploughgate of land, and all its other pertinents in pure and perpetual alms. Wherefore I will that the same monks should possess the aforesaid church and all that belongs to it, freely, quitely and wholly for ever. And they should have its multure entirely quit; and whosoever might hold the aforesaid church of them. I have promised however that according to my ability I shall maintain this alms for the benefit and use of the church of Durham.
